description: Standard: two drafts of a news article

input.version_a: The city council voted Tuesday to approve a $25 million bond measure for road repairs. The measure passed 6-1, with Councilwoman Janet Torres casting the only opposing vote. Torres said she believed the project was 'poorly planned and rushed.' The bond will be repaid through a property tax increase of $45 per year for the average homeowner over 15 years.
input.version_b: The city council approved a $25 million bond for road repairs Tuesday in a 6-1 vote. Councilwoman Janet Torres, who cast the sole dissenting vote, called the plan 'poorly planned' and warned that residents had not been given enough time to review the proposal. The bond will be repaid through a property tax surcharge averaging $45 annually per homeowner. Construction is expected to begin in September.
input.context: Editor asked the writer to add a timeline and tighten the lede

input.version_b: The report was published on Tuesday.

input.version_a: The European Commission proposed new rules on artificial intelligence on Wednesday that would ban the use of facial recognition in public spaces across all 27 member states. Commissioner Thierry Breton said the proposal reflected Europe's commitment to 'trustworthy AI.' Industry groups warned the rules would put European companies at a competitive disadvantage.
input.version_b: The European Commission on Wednesday proposed sweeping new rules on artificial intelligence, including a ban on real-time facial recognition in public spaces. The proposal, which must be approved by the European Parliament and member state governments, reflects what Commissioner Thierry Breton called Europe's commitment to 'trustworthy AI.' Industry lobby groups, including DigitalEurope and CCIA Europe, warned the rules risked putting European tech companies at a disadvantage against US and Chinese competitors.
input.context: Editor asked for more specificity on the legislative process and industry opposition
